title: Destructive confirm
summary: Typed-name confirmation flow for irreversible actions (delete account, drop table).
brief: Guard an irreversible action (delete project, remove user) behind a confirmation dialog. The destructive button is disabled until the user types the resource name.

steps.reason: Modal that blocks other interaction until resolved
Destructive-variant CTA to open the dialog

checklist.check: Confirm button stays disabled until the input matches the resource name exactly.
Confirm button uses Button variant='destructive', not default.
Cancel is the default/focus action, not Confirm.
[Alert Dialog] Avoid: Using Dialog when AlertDialog is semantically required
[Alert Dialog] Avoid: Omitting AlertDialogCancel (user must have an escape hatch)
[Alert Dialog] Avoid: Putting more than one AlertDialogAction (the pattern expects one destructive action)
[Alert Dialog] Avoid: Making the action button non-destructive styled

[Alert Dialog] A11y: Radix sets role='alertdialog', traps focus, focuses AlertDialogCancel by default, and closes on Escape. Clicks outside the dialog are prevented (user must choose Cancel or Action).
[Button] Avoid: Using 'destructive' variant for non-destructive actions
[Button] Avoid: Nesting interactive elements inside asChild button
[Button] Avoid: Missing aria-label when using icon-only size='icon' variant
[Button] Avoid: Using onClick for navigation instead of asChild with a link
[Button] A11y: Automatically handles focus ring, disabled state, and aria attributes. Icon-only buttons MUST have aria-label. Loading state automatically sets disabled.
[Input] Avoid: Missing associated Label element for accessibility
[Input] Avoid: Using type='number' without min/max constraints
[Input] Avoid: Not providing placeholder text for context
[Input] A11y: Always pair with a Label using htmlFor/id. Consider aria-describedby for helper text or error messages.
[Label] Avoid: Forgetting to set htmlFor matching the input's id
[Label] Avoid: Using Label for non-form text content
[Label] Avoid: Nesting interactive elements inside Label
[Label] A11y: Clicking the label focuses the associated control. Automatically communicates the label to screen readers. Use htmlFor/id pairing, not nesting.
